name: org.machanism.machai:machai-cli
description:
Machai CLI is a Java (Spring Boot + Spring Shell) command-line application providing an interactive shell for the Machanism Machai ecosystem. It orchestrates AI-assisted workflows around project/library metadata generation (bindex), metadata registration to an external registry, semantic search for libraries, and AI-driven project assembly/scaffolding. It also integrates Ghostwriter processors to scan documents/source code and produce refactoring guidance, and provides an interactive "Act mode" to execute predefined actions/prompts.

Key capabilities:
- Persistent configuration management via machai.properties using org.machanism.macha.core.commons.configurator.PropertiesConfigurator (org.machanism.machai.cli.ConfigCommand). Common defaults include project directory (gw.projectDir), semantic-search score threshold (score), Ghostwriter scan directory (gw.scanDir), GenAI provider/model (gw.model / bindex model), and input logging flags.
- Bindex generation: org.machanism.machai.cli.BindexCommand generates bindex metadata by scanning a project directory using org.machanism.machai.bindex.BindexCreator. Supports update mode and configurable GenAI provider/model.
- Bindex registration: org.machanism.machai.cli.BindexCommand registers/uploads generated bindex metadata to an external registry endpoint via org.machanism.machai.bindex.BindexRegister. Supports update mode.
- Semantic pick (semantic search): org.machanism.machai.cli.AssembyCommand.pick uses org.machanism.machai.bindex.Picker to retrieve candidate org.machanism.machai.schema.Bindex entries for a user prompt, optionally reading the prompt from a text file; supports minimum similarity threshold.
- Project assembly/scaffolding: org.machanism.machai.cli.AssembyCommand.assembly uses org.machanism.machai.bindex.ApplicationAssembly to generate an output project from the selected/picked libraries; can reuse last pick results or perform a new pick; supports output directory, threshold, registry URL, model selection, and optional LLM input logging.
- Ad-hoc prompting: org.machanism.machai.cli.AssembyCommand.prompt sends a prompt to a configured GenAI provider (via org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.GenAIProviderManager) and applies function/tool injection via org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.FunctionToolsLoader; sets provider working directory.
- Ghostwriter guidance pipeline: org.machanism.machai.cli.GWCommand scans directories/files and runs org.machanism.machai.gw.processor.GuidanceProcessor / Ghostwriter with configurable model, system instructions, default guidance prompt, excludes list, concurrency, and optional logging of LLM request inputs.
- Act mode: org.machanism.machai.cli.ActCommand runs org.machanism.machai.gw.processor.ActProcessor to execute predefined actions/prompts interactively; builds a default prompt from command words; scans documents in a configured directory (gw.scanDir) or falls back to the project directory.
- Workspace cleanup: org.machanism.machai.cli.CleanCommand recursively removes Machai temporary folders named .machai from a directory tree.

Runtime/integration details:
- Spring Boot application entry point: org.machanism.machai.cli.MachaiCLI. At startup it optionally loads additional system properties from machai.properties (or a file specified by -Dconfig=/path/to/file).
- Logging via SLF4J + Logback; console logs INFO+ and rolling file logs DEBUG+ under ${machai.home:-.}/logs.
- Primary dependencies and integrations include Spring Shell (interactive CLI), Machai bindex-core, Machai ghostwriter, and GenAI provider abstraction in org.machanism.machai.ai.*.

This artifact is intended for local developer workstations and CI/CD scripting where interactive or scripted metadata generation, semantic discovery, and AI-assisted project assembly/refactoring workflows are needed.

constructors.description: Main entry point for starting the Machai CLI. Bootstraps Spring Boot and Spring Shell. On startup it loads additional JVM system properties from a machai.properties file, or from a file path specified by the -Dconfig system property, before starting the application.

examples.description: Starts the interactive Machai CLI shell using machai.properties if present.

constructors.description: Creates the Ghostwriter guidance command. A JLine LineReader is injected (typically by Spring Shell) and used to prompt for instructions/guidance interactively when CLI options are empty.

examples.snippet:
org.jline.reader.LineReader reader = null;
org.machanism.machai.cli.GWCommand cmd = new org.machanism.machai.cli.GWCommand(reader);
examples.description: Spring Shell typically provides the LineReader; the command uses it to ask for missing prompt inputs.

constructors.description: Creates the semantic pick and project assembly command. The injected JLine LineReader enables interactive prompt entry when a query is omitted.

examples.snippet:
org.jline.reader.LineReader reader = null;
org.machanism.machai.cli.AssembyCommand cmd = new org.machanism.machai.cli.AssembyCommand(reader);
examples.description: Spring constructs the command; LineReader is used to request prompts in interactive mode.

features.name: gw
features.signature: void gw(int threads, java.lang.String model, java.lang.String instructions, java.lang.String guidance, java.lang.String excludes, java.lang.Boolean logInputs, java.io.File projectDir, java.lang.String[] scanDirs)
features.description: Scans directories or files and runs the Ghostwriter guidance pipeline using the configured GenAI model. Resolves defaults from persistent configuration (machai.properties) via ConfigCommand, including project directory, model, system instructions, guidance prompt, and logging options. Supports concurrency via threads, exclusion lists, multiple scan directories, and optional logging of LLM request inputs.

examples.snippet:
org.machanism.machai.cli.GWCommand cmd = new org.machanism.machai.cli.GWCommand((org.jline.reader.LineReader) null);
cmd.gw(4, "OpenAI:gpt-5.1", "You are a strict code reviewer", "Refactor for clarity", "target,.git", java.lang.Boolean.TRUE, new java.io.File("."), new java.lang.String[]{"./src"});
examples.description: Runs Ghostwriter guidance processing on ./src using 4 threads, excluding target and .git, and logging LLM request inputs.

features.name: act
features.description: Runs Ghostwriter "Act mode" interactively using org.machanism.machai.gw.processor.ActProcessor. Joins the provided words into a default prompt, resolves project directory and model from persisted configuration, and scans documents in gw.scanDir (or the project directory) before executing the action/prompt. Supports logging of LLM request inputs via gw.logInputs configuration.

examples.snippet:
org.machanism.machai.cli.ActCommand cmd = new org.machanism.machai.cli.ActCommand();
cmd.act(new java.lang.String[]{"commit", "and", "push"});
examples.description: Executes the predefined "commit" action with extra prompt text "and push".

features.name: pick
features.signature: void pick(java.lang.String query, java.lang.String registerUrl, java.lang.Double score, java.lang.String model) throws java.io.IOException
features.description: Performs semantic search ("pick") for libraries matching a user prompt. Uses org.machanism.machai.bindex.Picker to retrieve candidate org.machanism.machai.schema.Bindex entries from a registry (optional) above a similarity threshold. The query may be a literal prompt or a file path to a text file containing the prompt; results are retained for subsequent assembly runs.

examples.snippet:
org.machanism.machai.cli.AssembyCommand cmd = new org.machanism.machai.cli.AssembyCommand((org.jline.reader.LineReader) null);
cmd.pick("Create a web app", "https://registry.example/api", 0.8d, "OpenAI:gpt-5.1");
examples.description: Finds candidate libraries in the registry matching the prompt, using a minimum similarity score of 0.8.

features.name: assembly
features.signature: void assembly(java.lang.String query, java.io.File dir, java.lang.String registerUrl, java.lang.Double score, java.lang.String model) throws java.io.IOException
features.description: Assembles/generates an output project using the currently picked library list (from the last pick) or performs a new pick if a query is provided. Delegates project generation to org.machanism.machai.bindex.ApplicationAssembly. Supports configuring output directory, similarity score threshold, registry URL, GenAI model, and optional LLM input logging.

examples.snippet:
org.machanism.machai.cli.AssembyCommand cmd = new org.machanism.machai.cli.AssembyCommand((org.jline.reader.LineReader) null);
cmd.assembly("Create a web app", new java.io.File("./out"), "https://registry.example/api", 0.8d, "OpenAI:gpt-5.1");
examples.description: Performs a pick for the prompt and then generates an assembled project into ./out.

features.name: bindex
features.signature: void bindex(java.io.File dir, boolean update, java.lang.String model) throws java.io.IOException
features.description: Generates bindex metadata files for a project directory by scanning sources and project descriptors. Delegates to org.machanism.machai.bindex.BindexCreator; supports update mode (update previously saved metadata) and configurable GenAI model selection.

examples.snippet:
org.machanism.machai.cli.BindexCommand cmd = new org.machanism.machai.cli.BindexCommand();
cmd.bindex(new java.io.File("./my-project"), false, "OpenAI:gpt-5.1");
examples.description: Scans ./my-project and generates bindex metadata using the specified model.

features.name: register
features.signature: void register(java.io.File dir, java.lang.String registerUrl, boolean update, java.lang.String model) throws java.io.IOException
features.description: Registers (uploads) generated bindex metadata for a project directory into an external registry service. Delegates to org.machanism.machai.bindex.BindexRegister; supports update mode and configurable GenAI model selection.

examples.snippet:
org.machanism.machai.cli.BindexCommand cmd = new org.machanism.machai.cli.BindexCommand();
cmd.register(new java.io.File("./my-project"), "https://registry.example/api", true, "OpenAI:gpt-5.1");
examples.description: Uploads bindex metadata from ./my-project to the registry, updating existing entries.

features.name: prompt
features.signature: void prompt(java.lang.String query, java.lang.String chatModel, java.io.File dir)
features.description: Sends an ad-hoc user prompt to the configured GenAI provider and prints the response. Applies function/tool injection to the provider via org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.FunctionToolsLoader and sets the provider working directory. Useful for obtaining AI guidance outside the scanning/assembly workflows.

examples.snippet:
org.machanism.machai.cli.AssembyCommand cmd = new org.machanism.machai.cli.AssembyCommand((org.jline.reader.LineReader) null);
cmd.prompt("Suggest an architecture for a small Spring Boot REST service", "OpenAI:gpt-5.1", new java.io.File("."));
examples.description: Asks the selected GenAI model for architectural guidance with the current directory as working dir.

features.name: clean
features.description: Recursively removes all Machai temporary directories named .machai from a directory tree. Intended to clean intermediate artifacts produced by Machai workflows.

examples.snippet:
org.machanism.machai.cli.CleanCommand cmd = new org.machanism.machai.cli.CleanCommand();
cmd.clean(new java.io.File("./my-project"));

features.name: set
features.signature: void set(java.lang.String key, java.lang.String value) throws java.io.IOException
features.description: Sets or gets a persistent CLI configuration property stored in machai.properties. If value is null, prints the current property value; otherwise sets and saves it. Used by other commands to resolve defaults for directories, models, thresholds, prompts, and logging behavior.

examples.description: Configures the default GenAI model and default project directory in machai.properties.
